skill.name: @azure/storage-file-share (TypeScript/JavaScript)
skill.description: Azure File Share JavaScript/TypeScript SDK reference for SMB file share operations. Includes examples for creating shares, managing directories, uploading and downloading files, and handling file metadata.
skill.summary: Reference documentation for Azure File Share SDK with TypeScript examples
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. Contains TypeScript examples for Azure SDK usage. No security risks detected. All code examples are reference documentation demonstrating legitimate Azure Storage File Share operations.

content.user_title: Manage Azure File Shares with TypeScript
content.value_statement: Simplify Azure File Share integration with ready-to-use TypeScript examples. This reference skill provides code patterns for SMB file operations, authentication, and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Provide TypeScript code examples for Azure File Share SDK operations
Demonstrate authentication methods including connection strings and DefaultAzureCredential
Show file upload and download patterns with streams and buffers
Explain directory and file management operations in Azure Storage
Illustrate SAS token generation for secure access
Document error handling patterns for REST API calls
limitations: Some features like SAS generation only work in Node.js not browser
Does not execute code - provides reference documentation only
Requires valid Azure Storage account credentials to use examples

use_cases.title: Backend developer integrating Azure File Storage
use_cases.description: A developer building a Node.js application needs to store and retrieve files from Azure File Shares using SMB protocol. They need quick reference code for authentication and file operations.

use_cases.title: DevOps engineer configuring cloud storage
use_cases.description: An engineer setting up automated workflows that interact with Azure File Shares. They need examples for creating shares, setting quotas, and managing directories programmatically.

use_cases.title: Full-stack developer migrating file storage
use_cases.description: A developer migrating from local file systems to Azure Storage File Shares. They need guidance on upload patterns, stream handling, and error handling for production use.

prompt_templates.title: Basic file upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to upload a file to Azure File Share using TypeScript
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner getting started with Azure File Share operations

prompt_templates.title: List directory contents
prompt_templates.prompt: Provide code to list all files and directories in an Azure File Share directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring file share contents and structure

prompt_templates.title: Stream large file upload
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I upload a large file using streams to Azure File Share in Node.js?

prompt_templates.title: Generate SAS token
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to generate a SAS token for secure file access with specific permissions
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing secure, time-limited access to files

output_examples.input: Show me how to create a file share and upload a file
output_examples.output: Create a ShareServiceClient using your connection string, get a ShareClient for the share name, call create() method, then get a FileClient and use uploadRange() to upload content.

output_examples.input: How do I download a file from Azure File Share?
output_examples.output: Use the ShareFileClient.download() method to get a downloadable stream, then read the chunks from the readableStreamBody property and concatenate them into a Buffer.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production environments instead of hardcoded credentials
Set quotas on shares to prevent unexpected storage costs from unlimited file growth
Use streaming methods for large files to avoid loading entire files into memory
Handle RestError exceptions by checking statusCode for specific error conditions
anti_patterns: Never hardcode storage account keys or connection strings in source code
Avoid using uploadRange for large files when uploadStream is more efficient
Do not skip error handling - Azure operations can fail with network or authentication issues
Never log or expose SAS tokens or storage account keys in error messages

faq.question: What authentication method should I use?
faq.answer: Use connection strings for local development. For production, use DefaultAzureCredential with managed identities for secure, keyless authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in browser applications?
faq.answer: Some features like SAS generation and file operations are Node.js only. Browser applications must use pre-generated SAS tokens for authentication.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads?
faq.answer: Use the uploadStream() method with configurable buffer size and concurrency for files larger than 256MB to avoid memory issues.

faq.question: What is the difference between ShareClient and ShareDirectoryClient?
faq.answer: ShareClient operates at the share level for quotas and snapshots. ShareDirectoryClient handles directory and file operations within a share.

faq.question: How do I implement error handling?
faq.answer: Catch RestError exceptions and check the statusCode property. Handle 404 for not found, 409 for conflicts, and 403 for access denied errors.

faq.question: Can I create snapshots of my file shares?
faq.answer: Yes, use shareClient.createSnapshot() to create point-in-time snapshots. Access snapshots with withSnapshot() method and delete with deleteSnapshots option.
